steps.title: Follow the timeline
steps.content: The passage moves through Shakespeare's career chronologically: arriving in London as early as 1585, becoming known by 1596, and delighting audiences in 1605. The transition must signal a later point in time.

steps.title: Reject wrong relations
steps.content: A signals contrast, but the 1605 detail does not contradict the 1596 detail. C signals restatement, and D signals cause and effect; neither relationship fits the sequence. The remaining transition marks temporal progression. The answer is B.
